channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colleges say the Trump administration is using new tactics to expel international students
item.description: A crackdown on foreign students is alarming colleges, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Annie Ma And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rally and court hearing set for Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison
item.description: HYATTSVILLE, Md. (AP) — Lawyers for a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador will ask a federal judge on Friday to order the Trump Administration to return him to the U.S. The White House already has argued against the idea in legal briefs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stock market tumble impacts retirement savings; Florida economists explain
item.description: “This panic, or phase, is not going to last more than 30 days. The stock market, sooner than later, will bounce back,” said Tampa Economist Federico Alves.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dog saved after falling over 100 feet at Colorado National Monument
item.description: What started as a dog owner's worst nightmare ended in a heartwarming reunion thanks in part to a multi-agency effort by Colorado responders.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KRON4

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: At least seven people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs a 'double-edged sword': Sen. Ron Johnson
item.description: Trump promised the tariffs will bring about "the golden age of America," but economists have warned a recession could happen.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Natchitoches, Louisiana student wins first place in national competition
item.description: Brandon Jordan, a student from Northwestern State University, had to troubleshoot a virtual computer for the competition.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers propose controversial medically-assisted suicide bill
item.description: Minnesota state lawmakers are re-introducing a controversial bill legalizing medically-assisted death, which is currently legal in 10 states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tornadoes, winds, flooding and hail: Arkansas wakes up to disastrous severe storm damage
item.description: Communities across Arkansas woke up to severe storm damage as the state continues to prepare for more potential severe weather later this week.
item.dc:creator: Cate Skinner, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: Violent storms and tornadoes have torn through cities from Oklahoma to Indiana during what could be a record period of deadly weather and flooding.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Real estate software company RealPage sues California city officials over rental algorithm ban
item.description: Real estate software company RealPage filed a federal lawsuit Wednesday against Berkeley, California — the latest city to try to block landlords from using algorithms when deciding rents. Officials in many cities claim the practice is anti-competitive and is driving up the price of housing.
item.dc:creator: R.J. RICO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plastic bag found in dead bottlenose dolphin's stomach
item.description: Tuesday, Mar. 25, 2025, a large, dead bottlenose dolphin was reported on the banks of Deep Creek in the Rachel Carson Estuarine Reserve in Beaufort.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Beachy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs plan: What we know - and don't
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday is set to unveil reciprocal tariffs on several nations during a White House event. He has dubbed April 2 as "Liberation Day" in honor of these tariffs, with the ceremony being promoted as an effort to "make America wealthy again.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KTLA 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, WGN 9

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Democratic-backed candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court defeated a challenger endorsed by President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk on Tuesday, touting her victory as a win against powerful interests and cementing a liberal majority for at least three more years.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McMahon meeting with House Democrats over Education Department layoffs
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon is having a meeting with House Democrats on Wednesday over their concerns surrounding the layoffs at the Department of Education, which she and President Trump have been vocal about trying to eliminate entirely. Rep. Mark Takano (Calif.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US judge orders Trump administration to restore legal aid to unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in California on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to temporarily restore legal aid to tens of thousands of migrant children who are in the United States without a parent or guardian.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: It is the first time the Justice Department has sought to bring the death penalty since President Donald Trump returned to office in January.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, WGN 9

item.title: A court is allowing the release of most records in the Gene Hackman death investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A court on Monday cleared the way for the release of investigative records from the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, as long as depictions of the deceased couple are blocked from view.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections faced its first legal challenges Monday as the Democratic National Committee and a pair of nonprofits filed two separate lawsuits calling it unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains found in California decades ago ID'd as missing New York 13-year-old
item.description: A 13-year-old girl who was found dead along a river in California's Santa Cruz County three decades ago was recently identified through DNA testing, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Apparent explosion, fire injures about a dozen in Detroit apartment building
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — About a dozen people have been injured, including six children, following an apparent explosion and fire Monday morning at a 2-story apartment building in Detroit. The explosion was reported about 4 a.m., according to fire officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
